description.en:
# Specific:


# General:
Every node should have a _label that provides a human-readable summary label for the node. This is intended not for end users, but for developers looking at the data. See:  https://linked.art/model/base/#core-properties

# Arches Note:


* BranchId: la-label
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:
In order to make data both consistent and able to be customized to specific scenarios, nodes can be classified with terms from controlled vocabularies. This field gives a way to be more specific about the sort of entity, while maintaining the core information using the ontology class. See: https://linked.art/model/base/#types-and-classifications

# Arches Note:


* BranchId: la-classification
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:
In many cases, current data does not support the level of specificity that the full ontology allows, or the information is simply best expressed in human-readable form. For example, instead of a completely modeled set of parts with materials, many museum collection management systems allow only a single human-readable string for the "medium" or "materials statement".  Any time that there is a description of the resource, with or without qualification as to the type of description, then this branch can be used to record the descriptive text. See: https://linked.art/model/base/#statements-about-a-resource

# Arches Note:


* BranchId: la-statement
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:
It is strongly recommended that every resource that should be rendered to an end user have at least one name. Different types of name can be distinguished using classifications. See: https://linked.art/model/base/#names

# Arches Note:
This node's content should be the value for the title descriptor function, when used for a resource model.

* BranchId: la-name
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:
In many cases, current data does not support the level of specificity that the full ontology allows, or the information is simply best expressed in human-readable form. For example, instead of a completely modeled set of parts with materials, many museum collection management systems allow only a single human-readable string for the "medium" or "materials statement".  Any time that there is a description of the resource, with or without qualification as to the type of description, then this branch can be used to record the descriptive text. See: https://linked.art/model/base/#statements-about-a-resource

# Arches Note:


* BranchId: la-statement
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:
TimeSpan is used to record a span of time between two, possibly fuzzy, points. It can also have a known duration between those points.  For example, a 1 week activity might occur sometime between the first and third quarters of the year. See: https://linked.art/model/base/#time-span-details

# Arches Note:


* BranchId: la-timespan
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:
This branch can be used for activities associated with other entities, such as the production of an object, or the professional activity of a group or person. The class and relating property should be customized if possible.

# Arches Note:


* BranchId: la-activity-base
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:
Many resources have identifier values associated with them of different types, such as accession numbers, database numbers, lot numbers for auctions and so forth.  Identifiers are similar to Names, but instead use the Identifier class and do not have a language associated with them. See: https://linked.art/model/base/#identifiers

# Arches Note:


* BranchId: la-identifier
* FieldId:

# Specific:


# General:


# Arches Note:


* BranchId: 
* FieldId:

config.label: Source Reference Work for Statement about Creation Event

label.en: Source Reference Work for Statement about Creation Event

config.label: Source Reference Work for Creation Event

label.en: Source Reference Work for Creation Event

config.label: Related Event causal to Creation Event

label.en: Related Event causal to Creation Event

description.en: Collections or sets of objects. These might be curated as a museum or scientific study set, or simply sets put together for a particular purpose and then disbanded.

nodes.name: Identifier_type

nodes.description:
# Specific:


# General:
This branch allows the classifications themselves to be classified in order to know what sort of type it is, without recognizing all of them individually. This is important when the set of first degree types is not easily enumerable, such as classifications for the type of object or work.  See: https://linked.art/model/base/#types-of-types

# Arches Note:
Note that the metatype is hidden from view. Ensure that it has a default set when imported into another branch or model.

* BranchId: la-meta-classification
* FieldId:
